jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of episode with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic Homer exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfectly integrated character comedy but no setup required

jokes.text: Who's hospitalized with cirrhosis of the liver! Liver! Liver!
jokes.setup: Announcer promoting a benefit race for a daredevil
jokes.punchline: The benefit is for liver disease, darkly comic for a 'daredevil'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about a daredevil's unheroic medical condition

jokes.text: All right! Liver!
jokes.setup: Serious announcement about liver disease
jokes.punchline: Homer cheers enthusiastically for liver disease
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing the point and cheering inappropriately

jokes.text: It sure would be fun to carpool in one of those, huh, kids? I'd be a real hotrod mama, wouldn't I?
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on race cars
jokes.punchline: Marge's wholesome misunderstanding of race cars as carpooling vehicles
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge naivety - seeing practical family use in everything

jokes.text: Smithers, this beer isn't working. I don't feel any younger or funkier.
jokes.setup: Burns drinking beer at a sporting event
jokes.punchline: His expectation that beer should make him 'younger or funkier'
jokes.explanation: Burns' complete misunderstanding of youth culture and alcohol effects

jokes.text: Smithers, fetch the bi-oculars.
jokes.setup: Burns wants to watch the race better
jokes.punchline: Burns' pretentious mispronunciation of 'binoculars'
jokes.explanation: Burns' characteristic pompous speech patterns

jokes.text: Damnation! Tell them to go slower.
jokes.setup: Burns watching a car race
jokes.punchline: Burns wants the race cars to go slower, missing the entire point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - completely missing the point of racing

jokes.text: Could we keep it in first gear for a couple laps?
jokes.setup: Smithers following Burns' order to make cars go slower
jokes.punchline: Smithers actually asking race car drivers to slow down
jokes.explanation: Smithers' embarrassing devotion to Burns' ridiculous demands

jokes.text: This novelty foam hand is ludicrously oversized. Go swap it for a smaller one.
jokes.setup: Burns has a foam finger at the race
jokes.punchline: Burns missing the point of novelty foam fingers being oversized
jokes.explanation: Burns fundamentally misunderstanding novelty items

jokes.text: Hey, Burnsie! This was some swell shindig.
jokes.setup: Homer approaching Burns casually
jokes.punchline: Homer using outdated slang and treating Burns like a buddy
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual, inappropriate familiarity with his boss

jokes.text: Smithers! Help!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to shake Burns' hand enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Burns panicking and calling for help during a handshake
jokes.explanation: Burns' frailty and fear of human contact

jokes.text: You should've seen the murderous glint in his eyes, Smithers. And his breath reeked of beer and pretzled bread.
jokes.setup: Burns describing his encounter with Homer
jokes.punchline: Burns dramatically overreacting to a friendly handshake
jokes.explanation: Burns' paranoid interpretation of Homer's friendliness

jokes.text: I failed you, and I'll never forgive myself. Never! Never! Never!
jokes.setup: Smithers apologizing for the handshake incident
jokes.punchline: Smithers' dramatic over-the-top guilt over nothing
jokes.explanation: Smithers' excessive devotion and self-flagellation

jokes.text: I've alphabetized your breakfast.
jokes.setup: Smithers trying to make up for his failure
jokes.punchline: Alphabetizing breakfast is absurdly unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Smithers over-compensation

jokes.text: I've laminated today's newspaper.
jokes.setup: Smithers continuing his over-the-top service
jokes.punchline: Laminating a newspaper defeats its purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic that creates its own problem

jokes.text: How am I supposed to do the 'Junior Jumble'?
jokes.setup: Burns can't write on the laminated newspaper
jokes.punchline: Burns does children's word puzzles
jokes.explanation: Burns' secret childish habit revealed

jokes.text: Oh! I can't even grovel properly. I'm a buffoon.
jokes.setup: Smithers' attempts to help have backfired
jokes.punchline: Smithers acknowledging he's bad at groveling
jokes.explanation: Smithers' self-aware incompetence at his specialty

jokes.text: I don't deserve to live on your planet anymore!
jokes.setup: Smithers continuing his dramatic self-flagellation
jokes.punchline: Smithers treating Burns like he owns the planet
jokes.explanation: Smithers' worship of Burns taken to cosmic levels

jokes.text: I'm not a baby who needs a nursemaid to burp me.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming independence from Smithers
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific denial suggests he actually does need help burping
jokes.explanation: The specificity reveals Burns' actual dependence

jokes.text: You see?
jokes.setup: Burns demonstrating his independence
jokes.punchline: Burns presumably fails at whatever he just tried to do
jokes.explanation: Visual gag where Burns proves the opposite of what he intended

jokes.text: 714 names? Better be more specific. Lazy. Clumsy. Dimwitted. Monstrously ugly.
jokes.setup: Smithers searching employee files for 'incompetent'
jokes.punchline: The escalating insults and sheer number of incompetent employees
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of insults revealing workplace incompetence

jokes.text: Nuts to this. I'll just go get Homer Simpson.
jokes.setup: Smithers trying to find someone incompetent
jokes.punchline: Homer is the obvious choice without even checking files
jokes.explanation: Homer's reputation for incompetence is so established he doesn't need evaluation

jokes.text: I think Smithers picked me because of my motivational skills. Everyone always says they have to work a lot harder when I'm around.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he was chosen
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterpreting people working harder to compensate for his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely backward interpretation that reveals the truth

jokes.text: You know, Homer, assisting Mr. Burns could give your career a real shot in the arm.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving advice to Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately repeats the exact same line
jokes.explanation: Simple repetition gag showing family dynamics

jokes.text: Mom, I just said that. - Sor-ry.
jokes.setup: Marge repeating Lisa's advice
jokes.punchline: Marge's defensive sarcastic apology when called out
jokes.explanation: Realistic family awkwardness and Marge's passive-aggressive response

jokes.text: Next time, get your own darn corn.
jokes.setup: Marge being defensive about repeating Lisa
jokes.punchline: Marge escalating to a completely unrelated grievance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to petty domestic complaint

jokes.text: Um, is this the chair I'll be sitting on?
jokes.setup: Smithers explaining Homer's complex duties
jokes.punchline: Homer fixated on the most basic detail
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities are completely backwards

jokes.text: it's just 2,800 small jobs.
jokes.setup: Smithers reassuring Homer about the complexity
jokes.punchline: 2,800 jobs is an absurdly large number to call 'small'
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement of an impossible workload

jokes.text: moistening his eyeballs... assisting with his chewing and swallowing... lying to Congress
jokes.setup: Smithers listing Homer's duties
jokes.punchline: Escalating from bizarre personal care to major crimes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from intimate to criminal duties

jokes.text: Mr. Burns has a mother? She must be a hundred million years old.
jokes.setup: Homer learning Burns has a mother
jokes.punchline: Homer's characteristically absurd age estimate
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical mathematical exaggeration

jokes.text: She has limited capacities. All she can do is dial and yell.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about Burns' mother
jokes.punchline: Reducing an elderly person to just two basic functions
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny reduction of elderly capabilities

jokes.text: He never forgave her for having that affair with President Taft.
jokes.setup: Explaining Burns' family issues
jokes.punchline: Random historical figure in Burns family scandal
jokes.explanation: Great historical reference that fits Burns' impossible age

jokes.text: Taft, you old dog.
jokes.setup: Learning about the Taft affair
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual approval of a presidential affair
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate buddy-buddy attitude toward historical figures

jokes.text: I don't understand 2,700 of my new duties.
jokes.setup: Homer having trouble with his job
jokes.punchline: Homer understanding only 100 out of 2,800 duties
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's specific level of incompetence

jokes.text: Um, what do I do in case of fire?
jokes.setup: Smithers asking which duty is most problematic
jokes.punchline: Homer picks the most basic emergency procedure
jokes.explanation: Homer confused by the most basic safety concept

jokes.text: Good Lord, Smithers! You look atrocious. I thought I told you to take a vacation.
jokes.setup: Burns seeing someone he thinks is Smithers
jokes.punchline: Burns not recognizing Homer at all
jokes.explanation: Burns' inability to recognize his own employees

jokes.text: A single pillow of shredded wheat... some steamed toast and a dodo egg.
jokes.setup: Burns ordering lunch
jokes.punchline: Escalating from weird to impossible (extinct bird egg)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns order - pretentious and impossible

jokes.text: But I think the dodo went extinct-
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain the dodo problem
jokes.punchline: Homer surprisingly knows something, but gets cut off
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer knowing something, immediately dismissed

jokes.text: Um, can you repeat the part of the stuff... where you said all about the... things?
jokes.setup: Burns giving complex instructions
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete inability to process any information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - completely inarticulate confusion that's very quotable

jokes.text: Lousy two-legged pants.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with getting dressed
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming his pants for having the normal number of legs
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic - blaming pants for having two legs

jokes.text: Doughnuts? I told you I don't like ethnic food.
jokes.setup: Homer serving Burns breakfast
jokes.punchline: Burns considering doughnuts 'ethnic food'
jokes.explanation: Burns' bizarre categorization of common American food

jokes.text: Uh, they all won.
jokes.setup: Burns asking about his stocks
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of how stocks work
jokes.explanation: Homer treating stocks like a competition with winners

jokes.text: Well, you can either get up or go back to sleep.
jokes.setup: Burns asking about his options (financial)
jokes.punchline: Homer interpreting 'options' as morning choices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding of financial terminology as basic life choices

jokes.text: The accounting department is located on the third floor. Its hours are 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. The head of this department is a Mr. Johnson or Johnstone.
jokes.setup: Burns asking for a report on the accounting department
jokes.punchline: Homer's report is just basic directory information
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of 'report' as basic facts

jokes.text: You have 30 minutes to move your car. You have 10 minutes. Your car has been impounded. Your car has been crushed into a cube. You have 30 minutes to move your cube.
jokes.setup: Homer delivering phone messages
jokes.punchline: The escalating sequence from parking violation to cube-moving
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons escalation - perfect progression from mundane to absurd with great final twist

jokes.text: Is it about my cube?
jokes.setup: Phone ringing after the cube message
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate assumption it's cube-related
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback extending the cube absurdity

jokes.text: This Simpson fellow seems to be getting dumber by the minute. I've never seen anything quite like it.
jokes.setup: Burns commenting on Homer's performance
jokes.punchline: Burns treating Homer's stupidity as a fascinating phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Burns' clinical fascination with Homer's incompetence

jokes.text: Bart! Leave Simpson alone. - Simpson, I need a ride to the library.
jokes.setup: Family members treating Homer like an employee
jokes.punchline: Marge defending Homer then immediately asking for work favors
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy gag with Marge's contradictory behavior

jokes.text: Sixty watts? What do you think this is, a tanning salon?
jokes.setup: Burns complaining about various things Homer does
jokes.punchline: Burns treating normal lighting as excessively bright
jokes.explanation: Burns' extreme frailty and aversion to normal amenities

jokes.text: You're a travesty of a f- joke of an assistant!
jokes.setup: Burns' complaints reaching a breaking point
jokes.punchline: Burns so angry he can't even construct a coherent insult
jokes.explanation: Burns' rage causing him to stumble over his own elaborate insults

jokes.text: Except... I killed Mr. Burns!
jokes.setup: Homer seeming upset about work
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admitting to murder
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Homer's matter-of-fact delivery of shocking news

jokes.text: I punched Burns right in his 104-year-old face.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining what happened
jokes.punchline: Homer punching an extremely elderly man
jokes.explanation: Dark humor emphasizing Burns' extreme age and frailty

jokes.text: When I catch you, I'm gonna pull out your eyes and shove 'em down your pants... so you can watch me kick the crap outta you!
jokes.setup: Burns accidentally calling Moe's instead of Smithers
jokes.punchline: Moe's elaborate and physically impossible threat
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe threat with impossible but vivid imagery

jokes.text: Then I'm gonna use your tongue to paint my boat.
jokes.setup: Moe continuing his threat
jokes.punchline: Adding an even more absurd and specific threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with unexpected domestic detail

jokes.text: Cease your ingress.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to keep Homer away
jokes.punchline: Burns using unnecessarily fancy vocabulary for 'don't come in'
jokes.explanation: Burns' characteristic pompous vocabulary for simple concepts

jokes.text: which lever is the velocitator... and which the deceleratrix
jokes.setup: Burns trying to drive a car
jokes.punchline: Burns making up fancy names for gas and brake pedals
jokes.explanation: Great Burns vocabulary invention with pseudo-scientific terms

jokes.text: Beep-beep! Out of my way! I'm a motorist.
jokes.setup: Burns learning to drive and enjoying it
jokes.punchline: Burns' overly formal announcement of his driving status
jokes.explanation: Burns' formal way of announcing his participation in normal activities

jokes.text: Ahoy! Ahoy! No, you have the wrong number. This is 5246. I suspect you need more practice working your telephone machine.
jokes.setup: Burns answering the phone in his office
jokes.punchline: Burns using 'Ahoy' as phone greeting and calling it a 'telephone machine'
jokes.explanation: Burns' old-fashioned phone etiquette and condescending advice

jokes.text: You're fired. Ta.
jokes.setup: Burns reuniting with Smithers
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately firing Smithers with casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Burns' casual cruelty with British understatement

jokes.text: if you got fired from the- those things that... you do.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to empathize with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer having no idea what Marge actually does
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete ignorance of his wife's daily activities

jokes.text: We're gonna have to put a steel rod where your spine was.
jokes.setup: Smithers trying to get a piano moving job
jokes.punchline: Doctor's matter-of-fact description of catastrophic injury
jokes.explanation: Dark medical humor about inevitable injury

jokes.text: Do we need all those 'mayhems'? We do.
jokes.setup: Race announcer repeating 'mayhem' multiple times
jokes.punchline: Smithers questioning the repetition, getting shut down
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about announcer style with Smithers' practical concerns

jokes.text: Your job is to distract Barney until it's safely off the truck.
jokes.setup: Smithers asking about a job at Moe's
jokes.punchline: The job is specifically to prevent Barney from stealing beer
jokes.explanation: Specific job requirement based on Barney's alcoholism

jokes.text: I'll wager dollars to doughnuts... he still can't handle a call from his mother.
jokes.setup: Smithers planning to get his job back
jokes.punchline: Smithers knowing Burns' specific weakness
jokes.explanation: Smithers leveraging his intimate knowledge of Burns' fears

jokes.text: That improvident lackwit! Always too busy stridin' about his atom mill to call his own mother.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Burns angry about Montgomery not calling
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned insults and calling the power plant an 'atom mill'
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate language from extremely elderly character

jokes.text: I'll give him what for till he cries brassafrax!
jokes.setup: Mrs. Burns threatening to scold Montgomery
jokes.punchline: Made-up old-fashioned exclamation 'brassafrax'
jokes.explanation: Great invented old-timey exclamation

jokes.text: Um, sorry about pulling the plug on you and all.
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to be Burns talking to his mother
jokes.punchline: Casual apology for attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny casual reference to life support removal

jokes.text: Mrs. Burns is 122 years old, so try to sound more desiccated.
jokes.setup: Smithers coaching Homer to impersonate Burns' mother
jokes.punchline: Smithers uses 'desiccated' (dried up) as acting direction
jokes.explanation: Smithers' precise vocabulary for describing extreme aging

jokes.text: I just called to say I don't love you.
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to be Burns' mother
jokes.punchline: Inverting Stevie Wonder's romantic song into maternal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of famous love song into family dysfunction

jokes.text: Stop fighting like a girl, Simpson!
jokes.setup: Homer and Smithers fighting
jokes.punchline: Burns insulting Homer while Homer is winning the fight
jokes.explanation: Burns' outdated insult regardless of fight dynamics

jokes.text: Ring! Ring! Ring! - D'oh! D'oh! D'oh! - It's for you. - D'oh!
jokes.setup: Burns teaching Homer how to use a phone violently
jokes.punchline: Homer's repeated 'D'oh!' responses to physical abuse
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Homer's signature catchphrase

jokes.text: Ring! Ring! Ring! / D'oh! D'oh! D'oh! / It's for you. / D'oh!
jokes.setup: Burns and Homer fighting with phone
jokes.punchline: Burns uses phone as weapon while it rings, answers mid-fight
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with absurd phone etiquette during violence

jokes.text: Here comes the endangered condor into the power lines.
jokes.setup: News report playing in background
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact reporting of environmental disaster
jokes.explanation: Background environmental dark humor

jokes.text: I've got Bobo, hot from the dryer. Careful not to burn yourself on his eye.
jokes.setup: Smithers caring for Burns' teddy bear
jokes.punchline: Warning about burning yourself on a teddy bear's eye
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity in teddy bear care instructions

jokes.text: What'd you get that for? For knocking Mr. Burns out of a third-story window. Makes sense to me.
jokes.setup: Homer getting a medal
jokes.punchline: Lisa's casual acceptance of violence being rewarded
jokes.explanation: Lisa's pragmatic acceptance of the situation's absurdity
